companyResolutionResult.description: ADAMA Ltd. is one of the world's leading crop protection companies. We understand farming is much more than just a business, it’s a way of life.  We strive to Listen> Learn> Deliver. It’s as simple as that. Combining our passion for agriculture and our passion to succeed is what motivates us, and our best solutions come when we take your input and learn from your experiences. That’s how we transform the largest group of actives in the world into products you need. With one of the most comprehensive and diversified portfolios of differentiated, high-quality products, and state-of-the art R&D, manufacturing and formulation facilities, ADAMA's approximately 8,000 people reach farmers in over 100 countries across the globe, providing them with solutions to control weeds, insects and disease and improve their yields. For more information, visit us at www.adama.com and follow us on Twitter® at @AdamaAgri.
companyResolutionResult.name: ADAMA Ltd.

companyResolutionResult.description:
GCs for GCs | The Professional Home for In-House Counsel in Israel

Overview:
The GCs for GCs community is the largest and leading platform in Israel for in-house legal counsel. Since its establishment in 2021, the community has grown into an extensive network of approximately 3,500 professionals from all sectors of the economy—from top private and public companies to governmental and municipal entities.

Our Vision: Transforming professional isolation into shared leadership. Today, in-house counsel operate at a critical crossroads of decision-making. They are no longer just "gatekeepers," but strategic partners in executive management and on boards of directors. We are here to ensure you don't have to navigate this alone.

What We Offer:

Executive Networking: Direct connections with peers in similar positions for knowledge sharing, consultation, and collegiality.

Knowledge & Content Hubs: Dedicated professional forums based on areas of expertise (Tech, Pharma, Real Estate, Cyber, and more).

Innovation & Learning: Conferences, seminars, lectures, and "Legal-Talk" meetups that provide a practical toolkit for the modern era.

We believe that a strong community not only contributes to the personal development of the in-house counsel but also serves as a significant force multiplier for the entire organization.

Join the leading team in Israel's legal world:
🔗 Join the community: https://gcs-gcs.co.il/join_us/
📅 Follow our upcoming events and conferences: https://luma.com/gcsforgcs
companyResolutionResult.name: GCs for GCs

organizationUrnResolutionResult.description:
Approximately 36 years before Israel declared independence, a ceremony took place on the barren slopes of Mount Carmel near the Port of Haifa, which was then occupied by the Ottoman Empire. Unknown to the Haifa community witnessing the event, this would be a milestone in history. This first cornerstone embodied an implausible vision of creating a world-class institute of scientific and technological education in the Holy Land.
The story of the “Technikum” – the original German name of the Technion – is a tale of the century. The second industrial revolution created the printing presses and communications infrastructure allowing Jews scattered around the globe to organize in face of rising anti-Semitism. As Jews were often barred from technical education, the establishment of a technical school was a first priority to rebuilding a Jewish homeland. The Technion was to become a unique worldwide university that would precede, create, shape, and protect a modern state.
The cornerstone laid on April 11, 1912, set in motion a century of progress responding to national and global needs. Technion – Israel Institute of Technology would grow rapidly, becoming a global pioneer in fields such as biotechnology, stem cell research, space, computer science, nanotechnology, and energy. 
Four Technion professors have won Nobel Prizes. As it approaches its centennial in 2025 – 100 years after the first class took place – Technion City is a thriving world center of trailblazing excellence in research and teaching for the benefit of humanity, with 18 faculties, more than 14,000 students, 135 academic programs, and 60 research institutes. Since the Technion opened its doors, more than 90,000 Technion alumni have played a huge part in creating and leading the ‘Startup Nation,’ impacting the lives of millions of people worldwide.
organizationUrnResolutionResult.name: Technion - Israel Institute of Technology

organizationUrnResolutionResult.description:
In the academic environment in Israel, the Open University of Israel is similar to other universities in its pursuit of excellence and its commitment to superior scientific and scholastic standards. However, it differs in its organizational structure, its curriculum, and its admission requirements. 

The Open University is open to all those who wish to study a single course or a battery of courses, or to pursue a full program of study toward a Bachelor's degree. Enrollment does not require matriculation or any other certificate from another educational institution. Though applicants are not required to provide proof of prior scholastic achievements, their academic achievements are the key to their success at the Open University. Along with its open admission policy, the Open University demands of its students personal qualities without which academic achievements cannot be attained: ambition, motivation, perseverance, responsibility and self-discipline. Students are required to study on their own, practice at home, actively participate in group learning activities, and meet course deadlines and requirements. 

Since 1996, the Open University has offered programs of study leading to a Master's degree. In contrast to the open admission policy for undergraduate studies, admission to graduate studies is contingent on fulfillment of certain requirements. 
The Open University is flexible in structure and organization. Applicants joining the Open University student body are not required to enroll in a faculty or department, but rather in specific courses. An individual enrolled in a course becomes a student of the Open University. 

The structure of studies at the Open University also differs from the structure customary at other universities, as it is not defined by years. Open University students are not first, second or third year students. Student are entitled to determine their own rate of progress and the degree of progress in not measured by units of time ...
organizationUrnResolutionResult.name: The Open University of Israel

companyResolutionResult.description: Palo Alto Networks, the global cybersecurity leader, is shaping the cloud-centric future with technology that is transforming the way people and organizations operate. We help address the world's greatest security challenges with continuous innovation that seizes the latest breakthroughs in artificial intelligence, analytics, automation, and orchestration. By delivering an integrated platform and empowering a growing ecosystem of partners, we are at the forefront of protecting tens of thousands of organizations across clouds, networks, and mobile devices. Our vision is a world where each day is safer and more secure than the one before. For more information, visit www.paloaltonetworks.com.

organizationUrnResolutionResult.description:
ORT colleges are part of ORT Israel, which was established in 1949 and now includes 150 educational institutions. The network trains students to cope with the accelerated rate of development in the economy and in industry.

ORT colleges devise study curricula adapted to the needs of the country and to future needs, in collaboration with other bodies, such as: the Ministry of Science and Technology, the Ministry of Industry, Trade & Employment, the IDF, high-tech industries and other leading industries in the national economy, both in the private and public sectors.

Our graduates are integrated in the employment market in a wide range of key positions, in high-tech industries, other leading industries, government ministries, in the private market and in a large number of places of employment that require high quality professionals in the technological fields, at all levels.

ORT colleges operate as interdisciplinary education centers, ranging from science and technology studies to the arts, design and communications. The colleges aim to provide a solution for every student, enabling them to successfully meet local and global technological requirements.

organizationUrnResolutionResult.description:
Ben-Gurion University of the Negev is one of Israel’s leading research universities and among the world leaders in many fields. 

It has around 20,000 students and 4,000 faculty members in the Faculties of Engineering Sciences; Health Sciences; Natural Sciences; the Pinchas Sapir Faculty of Humanities and Social Sciences; the Guilford Glazer Faculty of Business and Management; the Joyce and Irving Goldman School of Medicine; the Kreitman School of Advanced Graduate Studies; and the Albert Katz International School for Desert Studies. More than 100,000 alumni play important roles in all areas of research and development, industry, health care, the economy, society, culture and education in Israel.

The University has three main campuses: The Marcus Family Campus in Beer-Sheva; the research campus at Sede Boqer and the Eilat Campus, and is home to national and multi-disciplinary research institutes: the National Institute for Biotechnology in the Negev; the National Institute of Solar Energy; the Ilse Katz Institute for Nanoscale Science and Technology; the Jacob Blaustein Institutes for Desert Research; the Ben-Gurion Research Institute for the Study of Israel & Zionism, and Heksherim - The Research Institute for Jewish and Israeli Literature and Culture.

The college deeply rooted in the local Galilean community. Braude College has established itself as a leading academic institution with worldwide academic and industry ties. The college plays an important role in the development and prosperity of the Galilee.

Mission 
 We are a higher education institution of engineering, technology, and related fields, 
 whose goal is to promote equal opportunities in Israeli society through education and 
 professional training. 

 Vision 
 A leading engineering degree in Israel.

 Braude College confers a Bachelor of Sciences (B.Sc.) degree in the following fields: 
 * Applied Mathematics 
 * Biotechnology Engineering
*Civil Engineering
 * Electrical and Electronic Engineering 
 * Industrial Engineering and Management 
 * Information Systems Engineering 
 * Mechanical Engineering 
 * Software Engineering 

Braude College confers a Master of Sciences (M.Sc.) degree in the following fields:

 * Industrial Engineering and Management 
 * Systems Engineering 
 * Software Engineering

companyResolutionResult.description:
Silverfort is on a mission to bring identity security everywhere, and allow organizations to operate without fear or disruptions. Fueled by a belief that enterprises and their identity teams deserve better, we found a way to break down the silos of identity security—eliminating the critical gaps and blind spots left behind by a patchwork of point solutions.

After years of research, we found a way to break free from these limitations. Silverfort created the only end-to-end identity security platform that secures all identities—humans and machines, on-prem and in the cloud. Our patented Runtime Access Protection (RAP) technology seamlessly integrates with the entire IAM infrastructure and secures it from within with unmatched visibility, analysis, and inline enforcement of security controls. This innovative approach brings protection to all resources in all environments, including ones that were previously impossible to secure, such as legacy systems, service accounts, command-line tools and IT/OT infrastructure. Finally, enterprises can stay ahead of the evolving identity threats, minimize their attack surface, stop breaches before they can spread, close compliance gaps, and break free from countless limitations and endless efforts.

Silverfort is the identity security platform the industry deserves, earning us the trust of more than 1,000 leading organizations, including several Fortune 50 companies. 

This is identity security done right.

Join Silverfort, and be part of a team that’s pushing the boundaries of identity security—no compromises, no blind spots, no limits.
companyResolutionResult.name: Silverfort

organizationUrnResolutionResult.description:
The Center of Academic Studies emphasizes the training of undergraduate and graduate students in professional work environments while maintaining a high level of academic studies, providing quality service and creating optimal conditions for the advancement of careers during the course of studies through an applied and practical curriculum. The Center supports the students in acquiring an education and academic training. To this end, the students benefit:
•    Up-to-date curricular.
•    Teaching staff that combines extensive academic knowledge with practical experience.
•    Accessible campus and a quality teaching environment.
•    Individual assistance and personal mentoring.
 
The Center for Academic Studies was established in 2008 and is located in Or Yehuda, a city that is a center for high-tech industries, higher education, and Israeli culture. From it's establishment to the present day, Or Yehuda has come a long way, and today the municipality is working to promote cooperation with various institutions of higher education, including the Center for Academic Studies, with the aim of increasing the number of higher education students in the city. As part of the cooperation between the Center and the city of Or Yehuda, students are active in community work, social contribution and promotion of the local community.
 
The Center for Academic Studies has five undergraduate and graduate schools:
•    "Serenat"​ management faculty
•    Faculty of Psychology
•    Faculty of Education
•    Faculty of Health Professions
•    The Faculty of High-Tech Studies

The Center aspires to be a leading institution in its field, combining modern academic education with a practical approach, and providing its graduates with the ability to successfully cope with the challenges of the practical world.

The Center advocates making higher education accessible to all people in Israel and promotes social involvement and responsibility.
organizationUrnResolutionResult.name: MLA - The center of Academic Studies

Project & PMO Leadership
- Lead the company’s PMO, driving project excellence using Salesforce, Smartsheet, and other key platforms.

- Align projects with customer expectations through active stakeholder engagement and consistent delivery.

Resource & Budget Management

- Oversee project budgets, track expenditures, and ensure financial accountability.
- Deliver regular budget and performance updates to stakeholders.

Risk & Quality Management

organizationUrnResolutionResult.description:
Ben-Gurion University of the Negev is one of Israel’s leading research universities and among the world leaders in many fields. 

It has around 20,000 students and 4,000 faculty members in the Faculties of Engineering Sciences; Health Sciences; Natural Sciences; the Pinchas Sapir Faculty of Humanities and Social Sciences; the Guilford Glazer Faculty of Business and Management; the Joyce and Irving Goldman School of Medicine; the Kreitman School of Advanced Graduate Studies; and the Albert Katz International School for Desert Studies. More than 100,000 alumni play important roles in all areas of research and development, industry, health care, the economy, society, culture and education in Israel.

The University has three main campuses: The Marcus Family Campus in Beer-Sheva; the research campus at Sede Boqer and the Eilat Campus, and is home to national and multi-disciplinary research institutes: the National Institute for Biotechnology in the Negev; the National Institute of Solar Energy; the Ilse Katz Institute for Nanoscale Science and Technology; the Jacob Blaustein Institutes for Desert Research; the Ben-Gurion Research Institute for the Study of Israel & Zionism, and Heksherim - The Research Institute for Jewish and Israeli Literature and Culture.

companyResolutionResult.description:
In today’s threat landscape, attackers use AI, automation, and stealth tactics to hide in plain sight—while SOCs drown in noisy alerts and reactive tools.
CyTwist changes the equation. Our Deductive-AI Profiler combines cyber-counterintelligence tradecraft with advanced Bayesian decisioning to detect attacker intent— not just indicators—within minutes of infiltration.

How CyTwist Breaks the Attack Chain
Uncovers AI-Generated Campaigns & Zero-Days
Detects never-before-seen malware, tactics, and fileless attacks—no signatures, no rules, no baselining needed.

Sees Through “Normal” Activity
Identifies stealthy lateral movement and living-off-the-land techniques that other tools overlook by analyzing attacker strategy, not surface-level anomalies.

Adds Urgency to Every Alert
Uses progression-based scoring and attacker intent modeling to prioritize only the threats that require action—drastically reducing false positives.

Deploys in Under 30 Minutes
Agentless and lightweight, CyTwist integrates with your existing logs and telemetry—no disruption, no tuning cycles.

Privacy-First by Design
CyTwist never uses your data to train shared models—fully aligned with GDPR and EU AI Act requirements.

The Result:
Your SOC moves from reactive triage to proactive interception—stopping stealth attacks before impact.
CyTwist. Deductive AI for the Stealth-Attack Era.
companyResolutionResult.name: CyTwist

Ironblocks is a pioneering cybersecurity firm that specializes in delivering comprehensive, end-to-end security solutions for the rapidly evolving Web3 ecosystem. Based in Tel Aviv, Ironblocks is driven by an elite team of founders and engineers who have honed their skills at the Israeli Defense Force OPSEC Intelligence Unit and leading tech firms including Bancor and eToro. 

The company's focus is on serving Web3 platforms, DeFi protocols, bridges, market makers, and various digital asset projects, providing a vital layer of security in the digital world. Leveraging the power of smart contracts, Ironblocks has developed an innovative solution that automates threat detection and protection. This approach allows for rapid preventative measures, capable of responding to threats in milliseconds, thereby offering an unprecedented level of security in the face of potential exploits. 

Ironblocks' mission is to be at the forefront of Web3 security, safeguarding digital assets and protocols, and ensuring the integrity of decentralized systems. Through its expertise and commitment to innovation, Ironblocks is shaping the future of security in the Web3 environment.

Reichman University's ten schools offer innovative, dynamic undergraduate and graduate programs that educate Israel's future business, juridical, high-tech, communications, economics, and civic leaders. 
All of the academic programs provide students with both a solid theoretical background and the practical interdisciplinary skills that are essential for success in the 21st century. 

The university’s alumni are leading key positions at government agencies, high-tech companies, and financial institutions. 

Reichman University is unique in its educational methods, which are based on an interdisciplinary approach and the teaching of information technology and global markets. It combines theory with real-world experience and provides its students with proficiencies.

currentPositions.companyName: Pixellot - AI-Automated Sports Video and Analytics

companyResolutionResult.description:
Today, millions of sporting events take place annually; however, 99% are not broadcast. These events range from local youth and women’s sports to lower leagues in popular sports and even professional leagues in niche sports. 

Pixellot’s automated sports production solutions revolutionize traditional video capture, production and distribution processes, enabling professional and amateur sports organizations to affordably cover and monetize their events. 
Pixellot’s technology streamlines the production workflow by deploying an unmanned multi-camera system in a single fixed rig (with additional angles as required), covering the entire field and delivering a stitched panoramic image. Advanced auto-production mechanisms track the flow of play, identify highlights, and insert advertisements without human intervention. Pixellot's analytics and video editing platform Vidswap allows coaches to save time by breaking down games and get detailed game, team and players stats 4 hours after the game is over. 

Over 40,000 Pixellot systems have been sold to production companies, sports portals, sports federations, leagues, clubs, venues, and coaching solution providers around the world, including the US, China, Germany, Spain, Netherlands, France, UK, Japan, Singapore, Mexico, and other markets.
companyResolutionResult.name: Pixellot - AI-Automated Sports Video and Analytics

organizationUrnResolutionResult.description:
In the academic environment in Israel, the Open University of Israel is similar to other universities in its pursuit of excellence and its commitment to superior scientific and scholastic standards. However, it differs in its organizational structure, its curriculum, and its admission requirements. 

The Open University is open to all those who wish to study a single course or a battery of courses, or to pursue a full program of study toward a Bachelor's degree. Enrollment does not require matriculation or any other certificate from another educational institution. Though applicants are not required to provide proof of prior scholastic achievements, their academic achievements are the key to their success at the Open University. Along with its open admission policy, the Open University demands of its students personal qualities without which academic achievements cannot be attained: ambition, motivation, perseverance, responsibility and self-discipline. Students are required to study on their own, practice at home, actively participate in group learning activities, and meet course deadlines and requirements. 

Since 1996, the Open University has offered programs of study leading to a Master's degree. In contrast to the open admission policy for undergraduate studies, admission to graduate studies is contingent on fulfillment of certain requirements. 
The Open University is flexible in structure and organization. Applicants joining the Open University student body are not required to enroll in a faculty or department, but rather in specific courses. An individual enrolled in a course becomes a student of the Open University. 

The structure of studies at the Open University also differs from the structure customary at other universities, as it is not defined by years. Open University students are not first, second or third year students. Student are entitled to determine their own rate of progress and the degree of progress in not measured by units of time ...
organizationUrnResolutionResult.name: The Open University of Israel
